content.Text: I’m checking the existing Vidly structure and the churn predictor pattern first so the new fraud detector matches the app’s conventions before I add files.
`rg` isn’t available here, so I’m falling back to targeted file reads. Next I’m pulling the churn predictor controller/view and the key model/repository types that the fraud service needs to match.

I have the controller and dashboard pattern. Now I’m pulling the rental/customer models, repository interfaces, and an existing service/model file so I can align names, enum usage, and project style before writing anything.

The project uses separate model/service files already. I’m checking the `.csproj` next because if it’s the classic MVC project format, new files may need explicit `Compile` and `Content` entries to build and render.

The project is classic MVC, so I’ll need explicit project entries for the new controller, service, model file, and view. I’m checking a few implementation details in the repositories so the fraud rules use the same data assumptions as the rest of the app.

One structural issue surfaced: the project file is explicit and may already be missing some newer feature files. I’m confirming whether `ChurnPredictor` itself is currently included, because that affects how aggressively I need to update `Vidly.csproj`.
